angles.label: CSS-only masonry (current state-of-art)
angles.query: CSS masonry layout 2024 2025 grid-template-rows masonry native browser support alternatives
angles.rationale: Targets the evolving CSS native masonry spec and polyfill alternatives (CSS columns, JS-free approaches) that are the primary implementation path for a variable-height card grid.

angles.label: Bento grid patterns & asymmetric card layouts
angles.query: bento grid layout CSS implementation featured card hero card asymmetric grid design system
angles.rationale: Bento grids with a dominant hero card plus secondary tiles directly address the featured-mod and category-spotlight layout patterns requested, distinct from uniform masonry.

angles.label: Card density toggles & view-mode switchers
angles.query: card grid density toggle compact comfortable spacious view switcher CSS UX pattern game mod browser
angles.rationale: Covers the specific UX interaction of letting users switch between high/low density card layouts, a distinct engineering and UX problem from the grid structure itself.

angles.label: UX research on card grid information hierarchy
angles.query: card UI information hierarchy UX research badge download count CTA placement high density grid readability
angles.rationale: Targets empirical or practitioner UX guidance on how to order and weight the five data fields (name, category badge, download count, description, button) inside each card for scannability at 291-card scale.

angles.label: Practitioner implementations in game/mod browser interfaces
angles.query: mod browser UI design CSS grid Nexus Mods CurseForge card layout implementation open source
angles.rationale: Grounds the abstract patterns in real shipped mod-browser products (Nexus Mods, CurseForge), surfacing production CSS decisions and community critiques directly applicable to a City of Heroes mod browser.

input.question: CSS grid mesh layouts for mod/card browsers — masonry, bento, asymmetric grids, card density patterns. Specifically for a City of Heroes mod browser with 291 mod cards showing name, category badge, download count, description, and download button. Looking for: modern CSS-only masonry approaches, bento grid patterns, card density toggles, featured/hero card layouts, and any UX research on card grid information hierarchy.
input.summary: Cover the space from four implementation angles (CSS-only masonry, bento grid patterns, card density/toggle UX, featured hero-card layouts) plus one UX-research angle on information hierarchy in high-density card grids, so results span both copy-paste code and design rationale a practitioner can combine for a 291-card mod browser.
